item.title: Trust Region On-Policy Distillation
item.description: On-Policy Distillation (OPD) is a fundamental technique for efficient post-training of large language models (LLMs), with broad applications in agent learning, multi-task enhancement, and model compression. However, OPD training becomes...

item.title: World Models Meet Language Models: On the Complementarity of Concrete and Abstract Reasoning
item.description: World models and multimodal large language models (MLLMs) provide complementary capabilities for predicting future outcomes from static visual observations. World models can generate concrete visual rollouts of possible futures, while ML...

item.title: Benchmarking Visual State Tracking in Multimodal Video Understanding
item.description: Understanding a video requires more than recognizing isolated moments, as humans continuously track entities, states, and events over time. This capacity for visual state tracking is fundamental to video understanding, yet remains undere...

item.title: AutoMedBench: Towards Medical AutoResearch with Agentic AI Models
item.description: Autonomous agents are increasingly expected to support end-to-end medical-AI research workflows, moving beyond isolated prediction tasks or short-form clinical question answering. However, existing medical agent benchmarks primarily eval...

item.title: The Meta-Agent Challenge: Are Current Agents Capable of Autonomous Agent Development?
item.description: Current AI benchmarks evaluate agents on task execution within human-designed workflows. These evaluations fundamentally fail to measure a critical next-level capability: whether models can autonomously develop agent systems. We introduc...

item.title: KVarN: Variance-Normalized KV-Cache Quantization Mitigates Error Accumulation in Reasoning Tasks
item.description: Test-time scaling is a powerful approach to obtain better reasoning in large language models, but it becomes memory-bottlenecked during long-horizon decoding, as the KV-cache grows. KV-cache quantization can help improve this, but curren...

item.title: Mitigating Perceptual Judgment Bias in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge via Perceptual Perturbation and Reward Modeling
item.description: Recent multimodal large language models have demonstrated strong reasoning ability, yet their reliability as automated evaluators remains limited by a critical weakness: when visual evidence conflicts with textual cues, MLLM judges tend...

item.title: Domain-Specific Data Synthesis for LLMs via Minimal Sufficient Representation Learning
item.description: Large Language Models have demonstrated remarkable progress in general-purpose capabilities and can achieve strong performance in specific domains through fine-tuning on domain-specific data. However, acquiring high-quality data for targ...

item.title: Ψ-Bench: Evaluating Persona-Sensitive Influencing in Persuasive Dialogues
item.description: Personalization is a crucial capability of modern language agents. However, current research primarily positions personalized agents as passive responders to user preferences, limiting their ability to interact with users and provide sug...

item.title: A Multi-AI-agent Framework Enabling End-to-end Finite Element Analysis for Solid Mechanics Problems
item.description: Finite element analysis (FEA) is the most important numerical approach for solid mechanics. Challenges of FEA include a steep learning curve for entry-level users and potential false simulations due to incorrect definitions of key simula...

item.title: ClawHub Security Signals: When VirusTotal, Static Analysis, and SkillSpector Disagree
item.description: Agent skills extend AI agents with reusable instructions, tools, scripts, references, and workflows, establishing a security boundary distinct from both model safety and traditional package-malware detection. ClawHub Security Signals is...

item.title: SynCred-Bench: Benchmarking Synthetic Credibility in AI-Generated Visual Misinformation
item.description: Recent generative models can now produce visual artifacts with realistic embedded text and layouts, creating a new misinformation threat: synthetic credibility. We introduce SYNCRED-Bench, a benchmark of 600 AI-generated misinformation i...

item.title: Language Models Need Sleep: Learning to Self-Modify and Consolidate Memories
item.description: The past few decades have witnessed significant advances in the design of machine learning algorithms, from early studies on task-specific shallow models to more general deep Large Language Models (LLMs). Despite showing promising result...

item.title: Decentralized Instruction Tuning: Conflict-Aware Splitting and Weight Merging
item.description: Instruction tuning aligns large language models, including multimodal ones, with diverse user intents, but scaling to heterogeneous mixtures is hindered by gradient interference and bandwidth-heavy synchronization. We ask whether these t...

item.title: NVIDIA OmniDreams: Real-Time Generative World Model for Closed-Loop Autonomous Vehicle Simulation
item.description: As autonomous vehicle capabilities advance, the safe evaluation of driving policies in long-tail scenarios remains a critical bottleneck. In closed-loop simulation, the driving policy model actively interacts with the environment, where...

item.title: Adaptive Auto-Harness: Sustained Self-Improvement for Agentic System Deployment on Open-Ended Task Streams
item.description: Auto-harness systems such as A-Evolve, GEPA, and Meta-Harness improve LLM agents by optimizing prompts, skills, tools, memories, and supporting infrastructure from execution feedback, but they are typically evaluated on fixed offline ben...

item.title: PlatonicNav: Unveiling Semantic Correspondence in Navigation with Platonic Topological Maps
item.description: Embodied visual navigation, where an agent perceives a complex environment and acts to reach a goal from raw sensory input, underpins a wide range of applications such as household service robotics, assistive robotics, and large-scale au...

item.title: OmniOPD: Logit-Free On-Policy Distillation via Speculative Verification
item.description: On-Policy Distillation (OPD) trains a student model on its own generative trajectories under dense token-level feedback from a stronger teacher, mitigating both the off-policy distribution shift of Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) and the sp...

item.title: GRAIL: Gradient-Reweighted Advantages for Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards
item.description: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (e.g. GRPO) is now a common way to improve mathematical reasoning in Large Language Models (LLMs). However, current methods usually broadcast one sequence-level advantage to all tokens, or u...

item.title: AgentCL: Toward Rigorous Evaluation of Continual Learning in Language Agents
item.description: Language agents spend substantial inference time solving individual tasks, yet the experience acquired in one episode is often underutilized in future episodes. Continual learning expects an agent to accumulate reusable experience across...

item.title: Pressure-Testing Deception Probes in LLMs: Scaling, Robustness, and the Geometry of Deceptive Representations
item.description: Linear probes trained on LLM activations are increasingly proposed as deception-detection metrics, yet report AUROC exceeding 0.96 on clean benchmarks while collapsing under distributional shift. This paper systematically pressure-tests...

item.title: OCC-RAG: Optimal Cognitive Core for Faithful Question Answering
item.description: Recent progress in the development of language models has been defined by scale, with each generation absorbing more of the world's knowledge into its weights. However, many practical applications benefit more from robust reasoning than...

item.title: A Local Perturbation Theory for Cross-Domain Interference and Recovery in Multi-Domain RL
item.description: Reinforcement learning (RL) post-training improves large language models (LLMs) on individual domains such as mathematical reasoning, code generation, question answering, and creative writing (CW), but training on one domain often degrad...

item.title: MIRA: Mid-training Rubric Anchoring for Source-Aware Data Selection
item.description: Mid-training has become an important stage in modern LLM development, using large-scale curated mixtures to strengthen capabilities before final post-training. Its data selection problem is distinct: the data are optimized under a pretra...

item.title: Bootstrap Your Generator: Unpaired Visual Editing with Flow Matching
item.description: Modern generative models possess a deep understanding of visual content, yet training them for image editing typically requires massive datasets of paired examples. This limits scalability, especially for video editing where collecting p...

item.title: TRON: Targeted Rule-Verifiable Online Environments for Visual Reasoning RL
item.description: Reinforcement learning (RL) for visual reasoning needs scalable, verifiable, and controllable training signals. Existing visual RL post-training trains on static curated datasets, with fixed image-question-answer samples bounded by their...

item.title: Decoupled Residual Denoising Diffusion Models for Unified and Data Efficient Image-to-Image Translation
item.description: We propose Decoupled Residual Denoising Diffusion models (DRDD) for unified and data-efficient image-to-image (I2I) translation. While diffusion models have advanced I2I translation in terms of quality and diversity, we uncover a previou...

item.title: Small RL Controller, Large Language Model: RL-Guided Adaptive Sampling for Test-Time Scaling
item.description: Test-time scaling improves the reasoning performance of large language models but incurs substantial cost in both total computation and latency. Existing adaptive sampling methods partially mitigate this issue by dynamically deciding whe...

item.title: Value-Aware Stochastic KV Cache Eviction for Reasoning Models
item.description: Reasoning models improve accuracy through extended chains of thought, but their long outputs create a memory and compute bottleneck. KV cache eviction methods reduce this cost by evicting unimportant key-value pairs from the cache, yet t...

item.title: Stateful Visual Encoders for Vision-Language Models
item.description: Vision-language models (VLMs) are increasingly used in multi-image, multi-turn agentic settings where decisions depend on visual changes. However, in existing open-weight VLMs, visual comparisons happen only inside the language model, wh...

item.title: Ultralytics YOLO26: Unified Real-Time End-to-End Vision Models
item.description: Real-time vision demands models that are accurate, efficient, and simple to deploy across diverse hardware. The YOLO family has become widely deployed for this reason, yet most YOLO detectors still rely on non-maximum suppression at infe...

item.title: AURA: Action-Gated Memory for Robot Policies at Constant VRAM
item.description: The KV-cache is the right memory for datacenters but the wrong memory for robots. Datacenter inference batches many short requests and resets them, amortizing an attention cache across a crowd. Embodied agents instead run one long, non-r...

item.title: WALL-WM: Carving World Action Modeling at the Event Joints
item.description: WALL-WM is a World Action Model that shifts video-action learning from chunk-centric optimization to event-grounded Vision-Language-Action pretraining, using semantically coherent action events as the atomic unit of learning. Existing WA...

item.title: From Activation to Causality: Discovery of Causal Visual Representations in the Human Brain
item.description: Identifying which brain regions represent a visual concept in the human brain is a central challenge in neuroscience. Existing approaches have localized coarse functional regions (e.g., faces, places) through activation maximization, ide...

item.title: Humanoid-GPT: Scaling Data and Structure for Zero-Shot Motion Tracking
item.description: We introduce Humanoid-GPT, a GPT-style Transformer with causal attention trained on a billion-scale motion corpus for whole-body control. Unlike prior shallow MLP trackers constrained by scarce data and an agility-generalization trade-of...

item.title: PaddleOCR-VL-1.6: Expanding the Frontier of Document Parsing with Under-Optimized Region Refinement and Progressive Post-Training
item.description: We introduce PaddleOCR-VL-1.6, an upgraded compact document parsing model built upon PaddleOCR-VL-1.5. Although PaddleOCR-VL-1.5 establishes a strong 0.9B baseline, its remaining errors concentrate in under-optimized regions where model...

item.title: Diagnosing Harmful Continuation in Answer-Correct Long-CoT Training Traces
item.description: Long chain-of-thought (CoT) traces are widely used as supervision for reasoning-oriented LLM SFT, yet answer-correct traces can still lead to markedly different fine-tuning outcomes. We study post-conclusion continuation in answer-correc...

item.title: αDepth: Learning Single-Pass Soft Boundary Decomposition for Stereo Conversion
item.description: Accurately modeling soft boundaries, e.g., hair and defocus blur, is a fundamental challenge in stereo conversion due to the ambiguous blending of foreground and background. Existing depth models primarily predict single-layer depth, lea...

item.title: Conditional Hypothesis Generation for LLM-Based Text Analysis with Researcher-Specified Covariates
item.description: A core goal of computational social science is to discover interpretable differences in how language varies across outcomes of interest, such as political affiliation or instructional quality. Recent LLM-based hypothesis generation metho...

item.title: Prior Availability in Industrial Visual Sim-to-Real: A Review of CAD-Guided and CAD-Unavailable Regimes
item.description: Industrial visual sim-to-real is often described as transferring from synthetic images to real images, but industrial deployment usually involves a broader mismatch between available evidence and required decisions. A system may be built...

item.title: MERIT: Learning Disentangled Music Representations for Audio Similarity
item.description: Current music similarity models typically compute a single, monolithic score, entangling distinct musical dimensions like melody, rhythm, and timbre. This limits user control and interpretability, making it impossible to execute nuanced...

item.title: BA-T: An Iterative Transformer for Two-View Bundle Adjustment
item.description: Feed-forward models for 3D reconstruction have achieved strong performance using deep cross-view attention to exchange information across images. However, these approaches often depend on heavy decoder stacks and lack a structured mechan...
